mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that forces corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized consumer demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline months yield positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a monthly drop in industrial production below its three-month moving average signals supply-side constraints that erode corporate output and cause immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of supply contraction months during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0 percent while CPI remains above 2.5 percent, negative real rates compress equity discount rates and drive positive forward returns as capital rotates into risk assets.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 65 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods, a quarterly decline in real GDP growth below zero percent confirms economic contraction that accelerates earnings downgrades and produces negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of negative GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized economic acceleration that boosts corporate capacity utilization and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration months yield negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of historical cases.
